regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, winds are light from the west-northwest at 3 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation with minimal impact on shore excursions. By day three, easterly winds increase slightly to 15 knots with drizzle and a 34% rain probability in Miami, which may affect shore excursions and require some operational adjustments. Beyond 48 hours, the trend toward increased cloudiness and light rain near Miami and nearby ports suggests that vessels and passengers should stay prepared for variable conditions impacting outdoor activities.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, expect south winds near 6 knots and thunderstorms with a 17% rain probability impacting Nassau, Freeport, and Bimini, which may cause brief delays in embarkation and shore excursions due to overcast skies and occasional downpours. Passengers are advised to remain flexible with shore plans as weather dynamics evolve through this period.
